votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Fibonacci function and matches the Coq specification exactly.
The Python code implements the Fibonacci function exactly as defined in the Coq specification, and the pre/spec functions match the Coq definitions.
The Python code correctly implements the Fibonacci sequence and matches the Coq specification.

The Python fib function implements the same recursive Fibonacci definition as the Coq fib function: fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1, fib(n)=fib(n-2)+fib(n-1) for n>=2. The precondition is trivially True in both, and the spec checks if res equals fib(n) in both cases. For all concrete non-negative integer inputs, they produce the same results.

Python fib matches Coq fib on all natural numbers (same base cases and recurrence); precondition is always true and spec equates res to fib(n) in both, so results align for all n >= 0.
